# Anchor: security_no_dangerous_patterns

> Scope: scripts/**/*.py, scripts/**/*.js, scripts/**/*.sh

## What it does

Forbids dangerous code patterns across all framework scripts. The MCP server and proof plugins run in the user's project context, so they must avoid shell injection vectors, arbitrary code execution, and hardcoded secrets. All subprocess calls use argument lists (never shell=True), and no eval/exec or credential literals appear in source.

## Rules

- RULE-1: No `os.system()` calls in any Python file under scripts/
- RULE-2: No `subprocess` call with `shell=True` in any Python file under scripts/
- RULE-3: No `eval()` or `exec()` calls in any file under scripts/
- RULE-4: No hardcoded passwords, secrets, API keys, or token literals in any file under scripts/

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): `grep -r "os\.system(" scripts/` returns zero matches
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): `grep -r "shell\s*=\s*True" scripts/` returns zero matches
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): `grep -rE "\beval\(|\bexec\(" scripts/` returns zero matches
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): `grep -riE "password|secret|api_key|token\s*=\s*[\"']" scripts/` returns zero matches
